code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test04-SEP-2019Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, suggesting possible chronic low glucose. Elevated creatinine indicates impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible diabetic nephropathy or renal impairment, despite low blood glucose. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further evaluation of renal function, glycemic status, and possible underlying metabolic or renal pathology.
